title: HTTP/2 Frames, Streams, HPACK, Flow Control, and Go Boundaries · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent protocol team receives different fragmentation, peer, trust, timing, and failure constraints and transfers http/2 frames, streams, hpack, flow control, and go boundaries into a new HTTP/2 connection, stream, frame, and compression model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of HTTP/2 connection, stream, frame, and compression model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Trace connection preface, SETTINGS exchange and acknowledgement, frame header, payload length, type, flags, stream identifier, and connection errors.
Apply idle, open, half-closed, closed, reserved, reset, and connection shutdown transitions independently per stream.
Map pseudo-fields and regular fields, enforce ordering and connection-field rules, bound HPACK tables and header lists, and avoid indexing secrets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab H2 Preface Settings Frames
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab protocol team handles case 5867 in a signed webhook receiver behind an HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 gateway. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine HTTP/2 frame, stream, flow-control, and header-list bounds; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab H2 Preface Settings Frames.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace connection preface, settings exchange and acknowledgement, frame header, payload length, type, flags, stream identifier, and connection errors. For Commit to the mission model · Lab H2 Preface Settings Frames, keep H2 Preface Settings Frames connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab H2 Go Transfer Boundary
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: httpproto-h2-go-transfer-boundary-1c3ffl" that uses case 7537 to combine HTTP/2 frame, stream, flow-control, and header-list bounds. Browser code must not open sockets or listeners, negotiate TLS, contact networks or proxies, capture packets, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Go 1.26 protocol transfer gates.
